checks.a_analysis: The output text reflects the source document's content. While the metadata title is AI-generated and deviates from the actual document title, such additions are explicitly listed as acceptable changes in the instructions. No other instances of hallucinated body text or invented headings were found outside of the specified omitted sections.

Every phone number, address, and URL visible in the provided JSON output was cross-referenced with the source PDF. In Appendix A, all 41 phone numbers across pages 31-34 match the source exactly. The URLs mentioned in the source (e.g., nconemap.com) are either in omitted sections or correctly referenced in the context of the report. No fabricated contact details were identified.

Appendix B (found on page 35 of the source PDF) is completely missing from the output. The JSON contains Appendix A (pages 31-34) and then jumps to Appendix C (page 36). While there is an omission block earlier in the document (between the Executive Summary and Appendix A), there is no omission note or placeholder for the content specifically between Appendix A and Appendix C. This is a critical failure as an entire section of the report is lost.
checks.b_evidence_source: Appendix B: NC Geographic Information Coordinating Council Establishing Authority and Precedent... The North Carolina Geographic Information Coordinating Council was established by Senate Bill 895 in August 2001 and was incorporated in the General Statutes §143-725 through 143- 727.
checks.b_evidence_output: [Missing section between Appendix A (reading_order 281) and Appendix C (reading_order 291)]

checks.a_analysis: The tables in Appendix A and Appendix C were audited for row counts. Appendix A (pages 31-34) and Appendix C (pages 36-38) match the source row-for-row, with only minor reformatting adjustments (such as the 'Advisory Members' header row on page 34 being preserved). No material row loss was observed in any presented table.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Sections that are present in the output (Executive Summary, Appendix A, Appendix C) were checked for internal completeness. No significant paragraphs or sentences were found to be missing within these specific sections. The text content of the tables and the executive summary appears to be fully captured.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Wording changes between the source and output are limited to whitespace and newline handling, which is consistent with the acceptable reformatting rules. Numbers, dates, and technical terminology (e.g., 'NC 2013 Orthoimagery Seamlines', 'NCDOT Pavement Condition Survey') are accurately preserved.

checks.a_analysis: The output was reviewed for repeated text blocks or duplicated sections. No instances were found where content from the source PDF appears more times in the JSON than in the original document.

The heading hierarchy is inverted on the title page (Section 1). The main title 'The Power of Place' is correctly tagged as H1 (reading_order 3), but the text preceding it ('North Carolina' and 'Geographic Information Coordinating Council') are tagged as H2 (reading_order 1 and 2). This results in H2 subheadings appearing before and outside of the H1 parent heading, which is a structural nesting error for screen reader navigation.
checks.b_evidence_source: North Carolina / Geographic Information Coordinating Council / The Power of Place
checks.b_evidence_output: {"type": "heading", "level": 2, "text": "North Carolina"}, {"type": "heading", "level": 2, "text": "Geographic Information Coordinating Council"}, {"type": "heading", "level": 1, "text": "The Power of Place"}

checks.a_analysis: There are multiple structural issues in the tables. In Appendix C (Sections 37 and 38), the first row of data on each page is incorrectly identified as a header row ('is_header_row': true) even though they contain data values like 'NCDOT Pavement Condition Survey...' and 'Shellfish Growing Areas' rather than the actual column headers (TITLE, CUSTODIAN). Additionally, in Appendix A (Section 34), the row for 'Advisory Members' is rendered with only 1 cell ('colspan': 1) in a table defined by 3-column rows, creating a structural grid mismatch.
checks.b_evidence_source: Table rows in Appendix C (Pages 37, 38) and the separator row in Appendix A (Page 34).
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 37, table rows[0] is_header_row: true. Section 34, table rows[3] has 1 cell with colspan 1.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order follows the top-to-bottom flow of the source document across all evaluated sections, including the transition from title page to the executive summary and appendices.

Lists in the Executive Summary (Section 3) are correctly identified. The checkmark bullets are translated into unordered lists, and the relationship between the descriptive headings (e.g., 'NC OneMap:') and the list items is preserved through logical element ordering.

The output correctly handles text-based URLs found in the source. While some sections containing links were omitted from the provided JSON sample, no evidence of fabricated or broken links was found in the visible sections.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML document follows the visual organization of the source PDF. Major sections, headings, and figures are presented in the same logical order. Multi-column elements such as sidebars (e.g., introduction on page 6 or quotes on pages 16-17) have been logically integrated into the single-column linear flow of the HTML, which is a standard practice for responsive web design and does not impede meaning or organization. Page numbers and the 'Page left intentionally blank' message are correctly omitted from the accessible HTML output.

The tables in Appendix A (Council Members) and Appendix C (NC OneMap Updates) are rendered correctly in the HTML. They maintain the structural integrity of the original PDF tables, with clear columns and rows, and distinct headers. The content within cells is aligned properly and remains fully readable.

All meaningful images, logos, and figures from the source PDF are present in the HTML output. This includes the agency logo on the cover page, the introduction photo (page 6), the GIS community diagram (Figure 1, page 7), the organizational structure chart (Figure 2, page 8), the meeting photo (page 9), aerial imagery and maps (Figures 3, 4, 5, 7), and the geospatial portal screenshot (Figure 6). The images are rendered clearly and correspond to their placement in the source text.

There is no visual evidence of duplicated body content. Each paragraph, figure, and table appears only once in its designated section. The document title and headings appearing in the navigation/Table of Contents area as well as in the main body is an intentional accessibility feature and is not considered a defect.
